basics.name: Dipankar Srirag
basics.label: Researcher
basics.summary: A Master of Information Technology graduate from the University of New South Wales, with strong background in Natural Language Processing (NLP).

location.address: 217, CSE Building (K-17), University of New South Wales

work.name: University of New South Wales
work.summary: Build a benchmark for sentiment and sarcasm classification for under-represented dialects of English.
highlights: Develop better data sampling strategies to make the existing datasets more challenging for LLMs.
Design and execute experiments using all classes of LLMs, which involve in-context learning and parameter-efficient fine-tuning.

work.name: University of New South Wales
work.summary: Lead tutorials for the course–COMP9444: Deep Learning and Neural Networks.
highlights: Mentor student groups through their end-to-end artificial intelligence projects.
Achieve a broad agreement of 95% from feedback provided by 20 students.

education.institution: University of New South Wales, Sydney

publications.name: Predicting the Target Word of Game-playing Conversations using a Low-Rank Dialect Adapter for Decoder Model
publications.summary: Propose a novel adapter-based architecture to make pre-trained decoder models (Mistral and Gemma) robust to other dialects of English.

publications.name: Evaluating Dialect Robustness of Language Models via Conversation Understanding
publications.summary: Evaluate open-source (Llama, Mistral, and Gemma) and closed-source (GPT-3.5 and 4 Turbo) models on masked target word prediction of conversations from dialogue games.

projects.name: Comparative Analysis of Abstractive Summarisation Techniques
projects.summary: Evaluate the effectiveness of static and context-aware embeddings in dialogue summarisation, using custom Seq2Seq, BART-base, and FLAN-T5 models.
